skill.name: trade-show-budget-planner
skill.description: Plan trade show budgets, estimate exhibition costs, and calculate expected ROI. Use this skill when the user needs to build a trade show budget, figure out how much it costs to exhibit at a show, estimate ROI from attending or exhibiting, plan spending for an upcoming event, justify trade show investment to leadership, or decide whether a show is worth the money.
skill.summary: AI-powered tool for building trade show budgets, estimating exhibition costs, and calculating ROI with industry benchmarks.
skill.author: LensmorOfficial

security_audit.summary: All 34 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This skill contains only documentation and prompt templates - no executable code. The scanner incorrectly flagged: (1) 'EUR' currency symbols as weak cryptographic algorithms, (2) markdown code fences as shell backticks, (3) relative documentation links as path traversal, and (4) marketing URLs as hardcoded network endpoints. This is a legitimate business productivity tool for trade show budget planning.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (FALSE POSITIVE)
high_findings.description: Scanner incorrectly flags 'EUR' (Euro currency symbol) as weak cryptographic algorithm. Lines contain budget values like 'EUR 18,000' - standard currency notation, not cryptographic code.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern matching error - scanner confuses 'EUR' currency with cryptographic algorithm. This is clearly a budget document with Euro amounts.

high_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution (FALSE POSITIVE)
high_findings.description: Scanner incorrectly identifies markdown code fences (triple backticks) as shell backtick execution. These are documentation examples in markdown code blocks, not executable code.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown triple backticks are code fences for documentation examples, not shell backtick execution. No actual code execution occurs.

high_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequence (FALSE POSITIVE)
high_findings.description: Scanner flags relative documentation links (../trade-show-finder/) as path traversal. These are normal relative links to related skills in the documentation.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are documentation links to related skills, not file system path traversal vulnerabilities. No user input is processed.

high_findings.title: Hardcoded URL (FALSE POSITIVE)
high_findings.description: Scanner flags marketing URLs in documentation as hardcoded network endpoints. These are static marketing links (lensmor.com) in documentation, not network requests in executable code.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static URLs in documentation are marketing links, not code making network requests. No credential exfiltration or malicious network activity.

high_findings.title: High File Entropy (FALSE POSITIVE)
high_findings.description: Scanner flags high entropy in README.zh.md. This file contains Chinese UTF-8 characters which naturally have higher byte entropy, not encrypted or binary content.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Chinese UTF-8 text has higher character diversity but is plain text documentation, not encrypted content.

content.value_statement: This skill helps you build detailed trade show budgets with industry cost benchmarks and calculate expected ROI to justify exhibition investments.
actual_capabilities: Generate itemized budgets across 4 categories: space & infrastructure, travel & accommodation, marketing & collateral, staffing & operations
Calculate ROI projections with sensitivity analysis (conservative, base, optimistic scenarios)
Provide cost-per-lead and break-even calculations for trade show investments
Suggest budget optimization strategies based on experience level and goals
Export budgets in markdown, CSV, or executive summary formats
limitations: Does not access real-time venue pricing - uses industry benchmarks that may vary
ROI projections are estimates based on typical conversion rates
Cannot account for all variable costs like custom electrical needs or visa fees
Does not integrate with event registration systems or venue databases

use_cases.title: Budget Justification for Leadership
use_cases.description: Marketing managers use this to build ROI cases and get approval for trade show investments from finance and executives.

use_cases.title: Pre-Show Cost Planning
use_cases.description: Exhibitors plan detailed budgets before committing to booth sizes and understand total cost of participation.

use_cases.title: Comparing Trade Show Options
use_cases.description: Teams compare costs and potential ROI across multiple trade shows to decide which events are worth attending.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Budget Request
prompt_templates.prompt: How much would it cost to exhibit at [SHOW NAME] with a [SIZE] sqm booth and a team of [NUMBER] people?
prompt_templates.scenario: Get a quick cost estimate for a specific trade show

prompt_templates.title: Attendee Budget
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a budget for attending (not exhibiting) [NUMBER] trade shows in [REGION] this year. We are a [COMPANY TYPE] company.
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan costs for attending trade shows without a booth

prompt_templates.title: ROI Justification
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to justify our trade show budget to my boss. Help me build an ROI case for [SHOW NAME]. Our average deal size is $[AMOUNT].
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a business case for trade show investment

prompt_templates.title: Compare Exhibiting vs Attending
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the costs and benefits of exhibiting vs attending [SHOW NAME]. What is the break-even point for getting a booth?

output_examples.input: How much would it cost to exhibit at MEDICA with a 20sqm booth and a team of 4?
output_examples.output: A detailed budget breakdown showing EUR 55,600 total estimate with categories: Space & Infrastructure (EUR 30,200), Travel & Accommodation (EUR 11,200), Marketing & Collateral (EUR 3,800), Staffing & Operations (EUR 10,400). Plus ROI projection showing break-even at 2 deals.

output_examples.input: Is it worth getting a booth at CES? We sell consumer electronics, average deal is $25K.
output_examples.output: ROI analysis with conservative, base, and optimistic scenarios. Shows projected leads, conversion rates, and revenue based on CES attendance. Includes break-even analysis and recommendations for booth size based on goals.

best_practices: Always add a 10% contingency to the total budget for unexpected costs
Include both conservative and optimistic scenarios in ROI projections
Factor in opportunity cost of staff time when calculating total investment
Use pre-show marketing budget wisely - it drives more qualified traffic than booth aesthetics
anti_patterns: Don't assume first-time shows will meet optimistic ROI expectations
Avoid underspending on pre-show marketing - it's the #1 driver of booth traffic
Don't forget to account for hidden costs like electrical, internet, and mandatory services
Avoid custom booth builds for first-time exhibitors - start with shell scheme

faq.question: What information do I need to provide?
faq.answer: Provide the show name, booth size (if exhibiting), team size traveling, and your average deal value for ROI calculations.

faq.question: How accurate are the budget estimates?
faq.answer: Estimates use industry benchmarks ($300-600/sqm for major shows). Actual costs vary by venue, timing, and negotiated rates. Always confirm with venues.

faq.question: Can this help with attending-only (no booth)?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill creates simpler budgets for attendees covering registration, travel, hotel, and opportunity costs.

faq.question: How does ROI calculation work?
faq.answer: It projects leads based on booth size and show traffic, then applies typical conversion rates to estimate revenue and compare against total investment.

faq.question: What if I'm a first-time exhibitor?
faq.answer: The skill applies more conservative conversion rates for first-timers and recommends starting with shell scheme booths to minimize risk.

faq.question: Can I export the budget?
faq.answer: Yes, you can request output in markdown tables, CSV format, or as a 1-page executive summary for budget approval.
